title: Rapper Flavor Flav donates sports equipment to Cambridge youth center
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Flavor Flav donated sports equipment to Frisoli Youth Center in Cambridge in partnership with Harvard College and Franklin Sports.
The donation included basketballs, volleyballs, soccer balls, tennis balls, kickballs, and cornhole equipment for after-school programs.
Frisoli Youth Center serves 4th-8th graders and teens aged 14-19, and is one of five city-run centers in Cambridge.

title: Jaylen Brown has a candid answer when asked if he deserves NBA MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is averaging career-highs of 28.8 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists in the 2025-26 season.
The Celtics hold the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference with a 54-26 record as Jayson Tatum returns.
Despite impressive stats, Brown candidly admits he is not analytically favored for the NBA MVP award.

title: Special World Cup ticket sales shatter Commuter Rail records in first day
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Over 17,450 special $80 MBTA Commuter Rail tickets were sold on the first day for World Cup matches at Gillette Stadium.
Nearly 11,000 tickets were sold within the first hour for the first five World Cup matches at the rebranded Boston Stadium.
MBTA plans to transport up to 20,000 riders per match with 14 trains, leveraging recent network-wide upgrades.

title: The Boston Fleet are ready to take a sold-out TD Garden by storm
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Fleet will play their first game at a sold-out TD Garden against the Montreal Victoire on Saturday.
Fleet stars Megan Keller and Aerin Frankel recently celebrated Olympic golds, boosting interest in the team's historic TD Garden appearance.
Saturday's game marks one of the highest-attended in PWHL history, reflecting growing interest in women's professional hockey.

title: Fall River woman who led sex trafficking ring with 5 others pleads guilty
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christy Parker of Fall River pleaded guilty to sex trafficking charges involving two minors and one adult victim.
Parker led a trafficking ring operating in Massachusetts, Rhode Island, and South Carolina throughout 2023.
Parker faces up to life in prison and significant fines for her role in the sex trafficking conspiracy.

title: Police look to identify owners of 2 dogs found dead on South Shore
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Weymouth police are investigating two dead dogs found separately on South Shore, seeking their owners for closure.
A white Terrier-type dog was found dead at Whitmans Pond, with no identifying tags or microchip.
A suspiciously wrapped Pitbull mix was discovered on Lakeshore Road; authorities request public help for identification.

title: She’s running Boston in memory of her son, and for  necrotizing enterocolitis
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jennifer Canvasser, 42, from Davis, California, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her late son Micah.
Canvasser founded the NEC Society to combat necrotizing enterocolitis after Micah's death before his first birthday.
She will also present at the Pediatric Academic Societies Meeting in Boston post-marathon, focusing on post-diagnosis NEC care.

title: Braintree man sentenced to life in prison for murdering Salem State student 40 years ago
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, sentenced to life in prison for the 1986 murder of Salem State student Claire Gravel.
Claire Gravel was 20 and a sophomore when she was murdered; her body found on Route 128, Beverly in 1986.
Carey was indicted in 2022; DNA evidence from Gravel's clothing linked him to the crime decades later.

title: ‘It’s pure excitement’: What James Hagens said after first practice with Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, 19, is acclimating to the Boston Bruins after signing his entry-level contract.
Coach Marco Sturm is cautious about Hagens' debut, awaiting the right moment for his NHL entry.
Hagens could boost the Bruins' offense, particularly their struggling power play and third line.

title: Report: 76ers star Joel Embiid to undergo surgery for appendicitis
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: 76ers center Joel Embiid will undergo surgery for appendicitis, sidelining him during a crucial season period.
Embiid's absence leaves Philadelphia in seventh place, chasing a playoff spot with five regular-season games left.
The Celtics and 76ers each won two games in their regular-season series, with close matchups deciding most games.
